title: betula procurva subsp. schugnanica
intro: Betula procurva subsp. schugnanica, also known as Schugnan birch, is a subspecies of birch tree that is native to the Schugnan region in Tajikistan. It is a deciduous tree with a distinctive white bark and has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The leaves and bark of Betula procurva subsp. schugnanica have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their high content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
